uk: 10🕂 Преподобного отця нашого Антонія Печерського, Київського
Святих сорока п'ятьох мучеників, що в Нікополі Вірменському
Святих братів-мучеників Франциска, Мутія і Рафаїла і тих, що з ними вбиті в Дамаску.
saints.en: Ven. Anthony of the Caves of Kyiv. Sts. Forty-Five Martyrs of Nicopolis in Armenia. Sts. Brother-Martyrs Francis, Mutius & Raphael, and those with them

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир прп., Слава: прп, І нині: богородичний-догмат за гласом «Слава». Вхід. Прокімен дня і три читання прп: Муд. 3:1-9, Муд. 5:15-6:3, Прип. 2:1-9. Стиховня ціла прп., зі своїми приспівами. Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом «Слава». Тропар прп., Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прп. (2), Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних, за гласом тропаря прп. Катизма чергова з сідальними прп., також полієлей з величаннями. Степенна 4-го гласу, перший антифон. Прокімен, Єв. Мт. 43 зач.; 11:27-30. Пс. 50, і стихира прп. Канон Богородиці і прп. По 9-ій – світильний прп. Хвалитні стихири прп. – 4, Слава: прп., І нині: богород. за гласом Сл. По великому славослов'ї з трисвятим – тропар прп., Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби прп.
Часи: на всіх – тропар і кондак прп.
Літургія: все Служба – прп. Апостол і євангелія спочатку Ряд.: Ап. – 1 Кор. 139 зач.; 7, 35 – 8, 7. Єв. – Мт. 63 зач.; 15, 29-31, а відтак Прп.: Ап. – Гал. 213 зач.; 5, 22 – 6, 2. Єв. – Лк. 24 зач.; 6, 17-23.

troparion_en.text: Having departed from worldly tumults, / in leaving the world you followed Christ according to the Gospel. / You reached the quiet refuge of the Holy Mount Athos, / living there a life equal to the angels. / Therefore, with the blessing of the Fathers, / you came to the Kievan hills. / There having fulfilled a life loving of labors, / you illumined your homeland. / And having shown a multitude of monastics / the pathway leading to the heavenly kingdom, / you led them to Christ. / Beseech him, O Venerable Anthony, / that he may save our souls!

kontakion_en.text: From your youth you gave yourself to God whom you loved above all, O Venerable One; / and in love you followed after him with your whole soul. / Scorning the passing corruption of the world, you made a cave in the earth; / and in it you struggled nobly facing the snares of the invisible enemy, / while illumining the ends of the earth like a bright, shining sun. / Therefore, with rejoicing you entered into the heavenly chambers. / Standing now before the throne of the Master together with the angels, / remember us who honor your holy memory, / that we may cry out to you: Rejoice, Anthony our Holy Father!

matched_oca_saint: Venerable Anthony of the Kiev Far Caves, Founder of Monasticism in Russia

oca_saints.name: Venerable Anthony of the Kiev Far Caves, Founder of Monasticism in Russia

troparion.text: Having departed from worldly tumults, / in leaving the world you followed Christ according to the Gospel. / You reached the quiet refuge of the Holy Mount Athos, / living there a life equal to the angels. / Therefore, with the blessing of the Fathers, / you came to the Kievan hills. / There having fulfilled a life loving of labors, / you illumined your homeland. / And having shown a multitude of monastics / the pathway leading to the heavenly kingdom, / you led them to Christ. / Beseech him, O Venerable Anthony, / that he may save our souls!

kontakion.text: From your youth you gave yourself to God whom you loved above all, O Venerable One; / and in love you followed after him with your whole soul. / Scorning the passing corruption of the world, you made a cave in the earth; / and in it you struggled nobly facing the snares of the invisible enemy, / while illumining the ends of the earth like a bright, shining sun. / Therefore, with rejoicing you entered into the heavenly chambers. / Standing now before the throne of the Master together with the angels, / remember us who honor your holy memory, / that we may cry out to you: Rejoice, Anthony our Holy Father!

oca_saints.name: 45 Holy Martyrs at Nicopolis in Armenia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their suffering have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “As the first fruits of our nature...”)
You endured many torments for the sake of Christ, O Martyrs.  / You destroyed idolatrous polytheism and godless error by the power of Christ. /  Teach us to cry aloud with faith: Alleluia.

oca_saints.name: The Placing of the Honorable Robe of the Lord at Moscow

troparion.text: Today, let us the faithful resort to the divine and healing Robe of the Savior, our God,  /  Who was pleased to wear it on His body and to pour out His holy Blood on the Cross,  /  with which He redeemed us from slavery to the Enemy.  /  Therefore, with gratitude we cry out to Him:  /  “By Your sacred Robe, protect our land, our Hierarchs, our city, and all the people; /  and as the lover of mankind,  /  save our souls.”

kontakion.text: O Master, You bestowed Your sacred Robe as a divine treasure,  / and as a garment of incorruption, healing, and salvation for all people. /  For in it You were pleased to clothe the holy and Life-giving flesh of Your Incarnation.  / Receiving it with zeal, we solemnly celebrate,  / and with fear and love, we sing to our Benefactor, crying out to You, O Christ: /  “Preserve all Orthodox Christians, their Hierarchs, and all people in peace, /  according to Your great mercy.”

oca_saints.name: Venerable Silvanus the Schemamonk of the Kiev Far Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Silvanus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyr Apollonius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Apollonius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Bianor and Silvanus of Pisidia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Bianor and Silvanus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: 10,000 Martyred Fathers of the Deserts and Caves of Scete by the Impious Patriarch Theophilus of Alexandria

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Agathonike and his companions, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Νikόdēmos, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Νikόdēmos;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyr Nectarius of Saint Anne Skete on Mount Athos

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Nectarius, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text: As a venerable and proven ascetic, as an honorable and renowned martyr,  / and as a model of the desert life, O most exalted Nectarius, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Konevits

troparion.text: Like the sun rising in the east, O Sovereign Lady,  / your Icon has shone upon us with brightness, /  wondrously illuminating with the rays of miracles, all who approach it with faith and love, /  and devoutly pray before your glory in your Son and God. /  Glory to Him Who gave us this Icon through Arsenios. /  Glory to Him Who brought it from Novgorod.  /  Glory to Him Who, through it, grants healing to all.

oca_saints.name: Saint Joseph, and his companions, of Damascus

troparion.text: O faithful, let us honor the martyr of Christ, / the priest of the Church of Antioch, / who baptized the land, the churches, and the people of Syria, / in the word of the Lord, / in his blood and in the blood of his companions. / Being baptized, since his youth, by the light of the Gospel, / he labored, taught and kept the Church of Christ with her sheep. / Therefore, O Joseph the Damascene, / be our example and our protector / and our fervent intercessor with the Savior.
